examples.title: Stack Implementation using Python List
examples.description: Basic Stack ADT implementation using Python's built-in list with essential operations: push, pop, peek, isEmpty, and size
examples.explanation:
1. __init__ initializes an empty list as the underlying storage
2. is_empty checks if list has no elements (O(1))
3. push appends item to end of list (O(1) amortized)
4. pop removes and returns last item (O(1) amortized)
5. peek returns last item without removing (O(1))
6. size returns number of items (O(1))
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for all operations (amortized for push/pop)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of elements

examples.title: Stack Implementation using Linked List
examples.description: Stack ADT implemented from scratch using a custom Linked List node, demonstrating dynamic memory allocation and pointer manipulation
examples.code:
class Node:
    def __init__(self, data):
        self.data = data
        self.next = None

class LinkedStack:
    def __init__(self):
        self.top = None
        self._size = 0
    
    def is_empty(self):
        return self.top is None
    
    def push(self, data):
        new_node = Node(data)
        new_node.next = self.top
        self.top = new_node
        self._size += 1
    
    def pop(self):
        if self.is_empty():
            raise IndexError("Pop from empty stack")
        data = self.top.data
        self.top = self.top.next
        self._size -= 1
        return data
    
    def peek(self):
        if self.is_empty():
            raise IndexError("Peek from empty stack")
        return self.top.data
    
    def size(self):
        return self._size

# Demonstration
stack = LinkedStack()
stack.push('A')
stack.push('B')
stack.push('C')
print(f"Stack size: {stack.size()}")
print(f"Top element: {stack.peek()}")
print(f"Popped: {stack.pop()}")
print(f"Popped: {stack.pop()}")
print(f"Is empty: {stack.is_empty()}")
examples.explanation:
1. Node class creates individual elements with data and next pointer
2. push creates new node, points it to current top, updates top (O(1))
3. pop saves top data, moves top to next node, decrements size (O(1))
4. peek returns top data without modification (O(1))
5. This implementation grows and shrinks dynamically unlike array-based
6. No memory wastage as nodes are created/destroyed as needed
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of elements (each node stores data + pointer)

examples.title: Practical Application: Balanced Parentheses Checker
examples.description: Real-world application of Stack ADT - checking if parentheses, brackets, and braces are balanced in an expression using stack operations
examples.explanation:
1. Stack stores opening brackets as they appear
2. When closing bracket encountered, pop from stack and verify it matches
3. Expression is balanced if stack is empty at end (all brackets matched)
4. Uses dictionary for O(1) bracket pair lookup
5. Demonstrates practical use: syntax validation, expression evaluation
6. Time: O(n) where n is expression length - single pass through input
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is length of expression
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) in worst case (all opening brackets, e.g., "(((((")
